metadata.description: Anchor definitions for Linear A decipherment dependency tracking
metadata.methodology: See METHODOLOGY.md Part 2: The Anchor Hierarchy

anchor_linear_b_comparison.name: Linear B Phonetic Comparison
anchor_linear_b_comparison.description: Phonetic values derived from Linear B cognates where sign forms are identical or nearly identical
anchor_linear_b_comparison.basis: 87 Linear A signs have Linear B counterparts; phonetic values assumed to transfer
limitations: 123 Linear A signs have no Linear B equivalent
Minoan may have had phonemic distinctions Greek collapsed
Transfer assumes phonology unchanged across ~300 years
anchor_linear_b_comparison.falsification_condition: Consistent mismatches in toponymic anchors would invalidate

anchor_semitic_loan_layer.name: Semitic Loanword Layer
anchor_semitic_loan_layer.description: Administrative vocabulary shows Akkadian/West Semitic parallels (ku-ro ↔ *kull, SA-RA₂ ↔ *šarāku)
anchor_semitic_loan_layer.basis: Gordon (1966) identified consonant skeleton matches; 17.7% corpus support
limitations: Absence of triconsonantal morphology suggests loans, not genetic relationship
Limited to administrative domain
Depends on Linear B phonetic assumptions
anchor_semitic_loan_layer.falsification_condition: If KU-RO arithmetic verification fails consistently

anchor_luwian_morphology.name: Luwian Morphological Layer
anchor_luwian_morphology.description: Grammatical particles and suffixes show Luwian parallels (-JA ↔ -iya, WA ↔ quotative)
limitations: Morphological particles may be contact features, not genetic
Religious texts show higher Luwian affinity than administrative

anchor_toponym_phaistos.name: PA-I-TO = Phaistos
anchor_toponym_phaistos.description: Geographic toponym confirmed by Linear B parallel and site location
anchor_toponym_phaistos.basis: Linear B pa-i-to at Phaistos = Greek Φαιστός
limitations: Greek name may be re-analysis of Minoan original
Greeks arrived ~250 years after Linear A ceased
anchor_toponym_phaistos.falsification_condition: Virtually unfalsifiable without new archaeological evidence

anchor_toponym_kydonia.name: KU-DO-NI-JA = Kydonia
anchor_toponym_kydonia.description: Geographic toponym confirmed by Linear B parallel and Khania site
anchor_toponym_kydonia.basis: Linear B ku-do-ni-ja = Greek Κυδωνία (modern Khania)
limitations: Same as PA-I-TO limitations
anchor_toponym_kydonia.falsification_condition: Virtually unfalsifiable without new archaeological evidence

anchor_commodity_logograms.name: Commodity Logograms
anchor_commodity_logograms.description: Pictographic signs for grain, wine, oil, animals clearly identifiable
anchor_commodity_logograms.basis: GRA (grain stalk), VIN (vine), OLE (oil vessel) are pictographically transparent
limitations: Some variants (OLE+KI, OLE+MI) meanings unclear
Exact commodities may differ from Linear B usage
anchor_commodity_logograms.falsification_condition: Would require reinterpretation of all administrative texts

anchor_kuro_total.name: KU-RO = Total/Sum
anchor_kuro_total.description: KU-RO appears list-final with values matching preceding sums
anchor_kuro_total.basis: Arithmetic validation (11.4% verified via corpus_auditor.py); positional evidence
limitations: Only 11.4% arithmetically verified
Depends on fraction value assumptions
anchor_kuro_total.falsification_condition: If verified percentage drops below 5% or consistent mismatches found

anchor_kiro_deficit.name: KI-RO = Deficit/Category
anchor_kiro_deficit.description: KI-RO as 'deficit' or category marker in accounting contexts
limitations: Function appears to vary by context
HT-exclusive raises regional bias concerns
anchor_kiro_deficit.falsification_condition: If non-administrative contexts show inconsistent usage

anchor_ja_suffix.name: -JA Adjectival Suffix
anchor_ja_suffix.basis: 65.9% word-final position; consistent with ethnic/place adjectives
limitations: Single-hypothesis (Luwian) support
Alternative interpretations exist

anchor_me_suffix.name: -ME Nominal Suffix
anchor_me_suffix.description: -ME as detachable nominal suffix, proven by IOZa16 line-break evidence (JA-SA-SA-RA / ME split across lines)
anchor_me_suffix.basis: IOZa16 physical line-break proof; 14 distinct -ME words (24 total occ); base form JA-SA-SA-RA attested without suffix
limitations: Pre-Greek parallel (-mn-, -m- in substrate words) is typological, not proven genetic
Luwian -ma suffix is alternative explanation
DA-ME (4 occ) may have different morphological structure
anchor_me_suffix.falsification_condition: If -ME is shown to be integral to roots (not detachable) in new attestations, or if base forms consistently fail to appear without -ME

anchor_si_suffix.name: -SI Verbal/Adjectival Suffix
anchor_si_suffix.description: -SI as detachable suffix, proven by TLZa1 base form U-NA-KA-NA without -SI in same formulaic position
anchor_si_suffix.basis: TLZa1 U-NA-KA-NA (base) vs U-NA-KA-NA-SI (suffixed); 28 distinct -SI words (35 total occ); U-NA-RU-KA-NA-SI shows internal morphological complexity
limitations: Pre-Greek parallel (-ss- in toponyms) is typological, not proven genetic
Luwian -ssi suffix is alternative explanation
High hapax count among -SI words (25 of 28 forms)
anchor_si_suffix.falsification_condition: If -SI words consistently fail to show base forms without suffix, or if -SI position varies (not consistently word-final)

anchor_ni_vin.name: NI → VIN (wine marker)
anchor_ni_vin.description: NI functions as wine marker, 100% VIN specificity
anchor_ni_vin.basis: 77 occurrences, 8 sites, 100% VIN co-occurrence when commodity present
limitations: Single syllable — linguistic hypothesis testing not applicable
anchor_ni_vin.falsification_condition: NI appearing with non-VIN commodity in clear context

anchor_approx_vin.name: ≈ → VIN (wine abbreviation)
anchor_approx_vin.description: ≈ sign functions as VIN shorthand, 100% specificity
limitations: Low frequency

anchor_kunisu_gra.name: KU-NI-SU → GRA (grain commodity word)
anchor_kunisu_gra.description: KU-NI-SU is grain-specific commodity term, 100% GRA specificity
limitations: Single-site attestation (HT only)

anchor_ole_ligature.name: OLE ligature → OLE (oil)
anchor_ole_ligature.description: OLE ligature sign has 100% OLE specificity
limitations: Low frequency, limited site distribution

anchor_dame_gra.name: DA-ME → GRA (grain commodity word)
anchor_dame_gra.description: DA-ME is grain-specific commodity term, 100% GRA specificity
limitations: Low frequency, single-site attestation

anchor_kupa_gra.name: KU-PA → GRA (grain commodity word)
anchor_kupa_gra.description: KU-PA is grain-specific commodity term, 100% GRA specificity
limitations: Low frequency

anchor_cyp_ligature.name: CYP ligature → CYP (copper)
anchor_cyp_ligature.description: CYP ligature sign has 100% CYP specificity
limitations: Low frequency, single-site attestation
